title: Border districts of Rajasthan reeling under severe heatwave - News On AIR

full_text:
The border districts of Rajasthan are currently reeling under severe heatwave conditions. Temperatures at several places are being recorded between 45 and 47 degrees Celsius. Barmer was the hottest place in the state today, with the maximum temperature recorded at 48 point 3 degrees Celsius. Phalodi registered a temperature of 46 point 4 degrees Celsius. Besides this, temperatures crossed 45 degrees Celsius in Jaisalmer, Bikaner and Chittorgarh, while several other places recorded temperatures above 44 degrees Celsius.
The Director of the Meteorological Centre Jaipur told Akashvani that severe heatwave conditions are likely to continue in the western districts of the state for the next three to four days.
Meanwhile, north-eastern districts of the state witnessed rainfall during the afternoon. The showers provided slight relief to people from the intense heat.

full_text:
Scorching Rajasthan: Rising Temperatures Trigger Heatwave Alert
Rajasthan is experiencing intense heatwave conditions, with temperatures peaking at 48.3 degrees Celsius in Barmer. The meteorological department forecasts a rise in both maximum and minimum temperatures statewide. Major cities, including Jaipur, recorded temperatures above 41 degrees, and further heatwaves are expected in Jodhpur and Bikaner.
Heatwave conditions intensified across Rajasthan on Tuesday as parts of the state witnessed a soaring rise in temperatures. The Meteorological Department has predicted that upcoming days will see a marginal increase in both maximum and minimum temperatures.
Barmer emerged as the hottest area, registering a sweltering 48.3 degrees Celsius. Other areas such as Phalodi reached 46.4 degrees, followed by Jaisalmer at 45.8 degrees, Chittorgarh with 45.6 degrees, and Bikaner at 45 degrees. Jaipur, the state's capital, wasn't spared either, recording a peak of 43.2 degrees.
These extreme conditions suggest a looming heatwave threat for Jodhpur and Bikaner divisions, as temperatures across all major cities surpassed the 41 degrees mark. Residents are advised to take necessary precautions as the state braces for the intensified heat.
(With inputs from agencies.)

title: Heatwave conditions in Rajasthan; mercury to soar further, says MeT dept - newsdrum.in
full_text:
Jaipur, May 12 (PTI) Heatwave conditions prevailed in parts of Rajasthan on Tuesday as the MeT department said most parts of the state will see a slight increase in maximum and minimum temperatures in the coming days.
Barmer was recorded as the hottest place in Rajasthan with the maximum temperature of 48.3 degrees Celsius.
According to the MeT department, Phalodi recorded 46.4 degrees, followed by 45.8 degrees in Jaisalmer, 45.6 degrees in Chittorgarh and 45 degrees in Bikaner.
State capital Jaipur recorded 43.2 degrees. All major cities across the state recorded maximum temperature above 41 degrees, the department said.
Jodhpur and Bikaner divisions are likely to witness heatwaves in the coming days, it added. PTI AG PRK
